The '''Mobile Proton Torpedo Launcher-2a'''—also called the '''MPTL-2a'''—was a medium mobile [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launcher crawler [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] platform that could fire proton warheads from a nine-tubed [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|launch assembly]] deployed from its armored shell. Used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the MPTL-2a was developed to counter serious design flaws being exploited by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in its predecessor, the [[Mobile Proton Torpedo Launcher|MPTL-L]].

==Description==
[[File:MATL_UNPAKED.jpg|thumb|left|180px|An MPTL-2a packed to move, accompanied by a [[Longvision LV-38 spotter/probe droid|spotter droid]].]]
This track-treaded mobile weapons platform was designed and produced by [[Loratus Manufacturing]]. When ready to deploy [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], the armored shell of the vehicle would split open to expose nine [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|launch tubes]]. When used in coordination with [[Longvision LV-38 spotter/probe droid|spotter droids]] that [[sensor ping]]ed heavily armored enemy units, the MPTL-2a had a maximum firing range of more than 20 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]. However, range and accuracy could be severely hampered without assistance from sensor droids or spotting units.<ref name="db" />

==History==
Due to the slow responsiveness and turn rate of the [[Mobile Proton Torpedo Launcher|MPTL-L]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] units began exploiting its sluggishness during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] sent [[Loratus Manufacturing]] desperate requests for design upgrades, and the resulting 2a variant was lighter, making it more maneuverable and faster in deploying its launch assembly than the MPTL-L. However, the lightweight build hampered the launcher's stability and range, and the initial upgrades performed poorly during field tests, requiring the addition of [[gription]] stabilizers to steady the vehicle so the launch assembly could effectively fire. Although its increased mobility over the MPTL-L made it less of an easy target, it was not a direct combat vehicle. Therefore, Rebel [[Commander/Legends|Commanders]] attached armed escorts to the vehicles or kept them out of range of heavy fire. In spite of its few shortcomings, the MPTL-2a was a significant upgrade over the MPTL-L design due to its lightweight build and—with the accompaniment of spotter units—its extended weapons range.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
The MPTL-2a appears in ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' as the Rebel artillery unit.
